new_classes.definition_en: A material entity that functions as a sweetening agent added to a beverage, including sucrose, glucose, fructose, artificial sweeteners, and similar substances used to impart sweetness.

A material entity added to a water-based beverage to impart a characteristic flavour, including natural and artificial flavouring agents, quinine, vitamins, antioxidants, and stabilisers that do not change the classification of the product.

Water, including mineral water and aerated water, to which sugar, other sweetening matter, or flavouring has been added, classified under CN subheading 2202 10. Soft drinks as described in the HS Explanatory Note to heading 2202, paragraph (A). The presence of antioxidants, vitamins, stabilisers, or quinine does not affect classification.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the three primary distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) the water base (mineral water, aerated water, or plain water) modelled via a BFO has-part restriction to MineralOrCarbonatedWater; (2) the addition of sugar or other sweetening matter, modelled via hasAddedSweetener; and (3) the addition of flavouring substances, modelled via hasAddedFlavouring. The note that antioxidants, vitamins, stabilisers, and quinine do not affect classification is captured in the definition of FlavouringSubstance and in the class definition text. A minor gap remains: the legal text specifies that CN 2202 10 covers soft drinks as per HS Explanatory Note 2202(A), which references a broader category concept that is difficult to formalise exhaustively in OWL without the full HS EN text. Additionally, the disjunctive nature of the classification criterion (added sugar OR sweetening matter OR flavouring) is expressed as parallel someValuesFrom restrictions, which in OWL 2 DL represents a conjunction rather than a disjunction — this is a known modelling limitation. Plain water without additives is not captured as a complement/disjoint, which would require additional sibling axioms outside this node's scope.
